summary: Design or refresh a recruitment process with sourcing strategy, candidate evaluation, and human-review checkpoints.

inputs.name: role_requirements
inputs.description: Role definition, level, competencies, and hiring objective.

inputs.name: sourcing_context
inputs.description: Available sourcing channels, budget, and diversity goals.

inputs.name: policy_constraints
inputs.description: Internal policy, regional cautions, or compliance requirements.

outputs.name: recruitment_plan
outputs.description: Sourcing strategy, timeline, and channel plan.

outputs.name: evaluation_framework
outputs.description: Candidate assessment criteria, interview structure, and scoring rubric.

budget.notes: High-stakes workflow. Preserve fairness and human decision ownership.

gates.rule: Confirm the request is recruitment process design, not legal advice or autonomous hiring approval.
Require role requirements and sourcing context before drafting recruitment plan.
Mark outputs as decision-support artifacts and include a human manager or HR-owner checkpoint.
Return clear sourcing strategy and note where policy or offer decisions need human approval.

steps.action: Set role outcomes, competencies, and evaluation criteria.
Create sourcing plan with channel selection, diversity goals, and outreach messaging.
Design interview structure, scoring rubric, and candidate assessment criteria.
Merge sourcing strategy and evaluation framework into one reviewer-ready plan.

tools.name: filesystem.read
tools.purpose: Read role profiles, sourcing data, and policy references.

verification.method: Check the plan names role requirements before sourcing strategy.
Check sourcing channels include diverse pipeline sources.
Check policy, offer, or hiring decisions are explicitly human-owned.

success_criteria: The recruitment plan can be executed without guessing role criteria or sourcing strategy.
Evaluation guidance is structured and consistent enough to reduce ad hoc decision making.
The output clearly states that hiring and policy decisions remain with human managers.

rollback.when: If the plan omits role requirements or approval points, revert to role definition and rebuild.
